claim: Anthropic's official devcontainer ships an init-firewall.sh that uses iptables+ipset with a default-deny OUTPUT policy, allowlisting only GitHub ranges, registry.npmjs.org, api.anthropic.com, statsig.anthropic.com, sentry.io, VS Code marketplace, DNS/UDP 53 and SSH/TCP 22, then verifies by blocking example.com.
quote: iptables -P OUTPUT DROP ... iptables -A OUTPUT -m set --match-set allowed-domains dst -j ACCEPT ... iptables -A OUTPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-admin-prohibited

claim: The official devcontainer is based on node:20, runs as non-root user 'node', and installs zsh, git, gh, fzf, iptables/ipset/aggregate for the firewall, git-delta, and @anthropic-ai/claude-code globally.
quote: FROM node:20 ... iptables, ipset, iproute2, dnsutils, aggregate ... @anthropic-ai/claude-code ... Default Shell: /bin/zsh ... Working Directory: /workspace

claim: Anthropic's devcontainer.json does NOT pass --dangerously-skip-permissions; instead it adds NET_ADMIN/NET_RAW caps, mounts a volume at /home/node/.claude for auth persistence, and runs init-firewall.sh as postStartCommand.

HolyClaude is a single-maintainer (CoderLuii) AI coding workstation image with ~2.1k stars (April 2026, v1.2.2 released 2026-04-10) bundling 7 AI CLIs (Claude Code, Gemini, Codex, Cursor, TaskMaster, Junie, OpenCode) plus Node 22, Python 3, Playwright, Postgres/Redis/SQLite clients, FFmpeg, and a web UI on port 3001.
quote: AI coding workstation: Claude Code + web UI + 7 AI CLIs + headless browser + 50+ tools ... docker compose up -d ... http://localhost:3001

claim: HolyClaude requires SYS_ADMIN cap and seccomp=unconfined to run Chromium, uses s6-overlay for process supervision, persists OAuth tokens in a bind-mounted ./data/claude/ directory, and defaults to 'allowEdits' mode requiring confirmation rather than --dangerously-skip-permissions.
quote: s6-overlay instead of supervisord for graceful shutdown and signal forwarding ... OAuth tokens stored locally in bind-mounted ./data/claude/ directory

claim: claude-code-sandbox (textcortex) is archived as of February 2026 at ~308 stars; it was an npm CLI (claude-sandbox start) that ran Claude in Docker/Podman with files copied in (not mounted) and explicitly invoked --dangerously-skip-permissions with allowedTools defaulting to ['*'].
quote: This was an early PoC and is now archived. Follow Spritz for the continuation of this vision ... Claude runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions flag (safe in container)

claim: Sculptor by Imbue (~146 stars, still in active beta April 2026, released 2025-09-26) wraps Docker Desktop with a UI for running many parallel Claude Code agents in per-agent containers, using Mutagen for bidirectional 'Pairing Mode' sync between container and host IDE; OAuth sign-in for Pro/Max plans is post-beta.
quote: Sculptor is a UI for running parallel coding agents in safe, isolated sandboxes ... We plan on pushing out an update soon that allows Claude auth sign-in so you can use your Claude Pro and Max plans!

claim: Sculptor's defining feature is Pairing Mode, which one-clicks an agent's container work back into the developer's local repo while keeping files and git state synced bidirectionally, and currently runs on Mac (Apple Silicon) and Linux with Intel/Windows coming.
quote: With one click, Sculptor brings an agent's work from its container into your local repo, keeping your files and git state synced.

claim: Dagger's container-use (~3.7k stars, Go-based, v0.4.2 August 2025) is an MCP server that lets any MCP-compatible agent (Claude Code, Cursor) spin up per-task containers on a separate git branch via 'npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code mcp add container-use -- cu stdio'; environments are defined as Dagger composable functions, not static images.
quote: Each agent gets a fresh container in its own git branch - run multiple agents without conflicts, experiment safely, discard failures instantly.

claim: Docker Sandboxes (GA January 2026 on macOS/Windows, Linux coming) run each agent in a dedicated microVM with its own kernel and nested docker engine, launch Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions by default, and support Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Kiro out of the box.
quote: Each coding agent runs in a dedicated microVM with hypervisor-level security boundaries ... Host Docker daemon remains inaccessible to agents ... Unattended execution - No constant permission prompts

claim: Docker Sandboxes launch via a single 'sbx run claude ~/my-project' command, use 'docker/sandbox-templates:claude-code' as the base, handle auth either via 'sbx secret set' for API keys or an OAuth proxy outside the sandbox, but intentionally do NOT inherit the host's ~/.claude config.
quote: Sandboxes don't pick up user-level configuration from your host, such as ~/.claude. Only project-level configuration in the working directory is available inside the sandbox.

claim: Claude Code itself now has native OS-level sandboxing (macOS Seatbelt, Linux/WSL2 bubblewrap+socat) via the /sandbox command with filesystem and proxy-based network isolation, reducing permission prompts by 84% internally; on Linux inside Docker it needs enableWeakerNestedSandbox which 'considerably weakens security'.
quote: macOS: Uses Seatbelt for sandbox enforcement ... Linux: Uses bubblewrap for isolation ... Linux Sandbox Strength: ... enableWeakerNestedSandbox mode ... considerably weakens security

claim: claudebox by RchGrav (~1k stars, v2.0.0 July 2025) offers 15+ pre-baked profiles (C/C++, Python, Rust, Go, Java, ML, DevOps) with per-project isolation of Docker images, auth, and history under ~/.claudebox/<project>/.claude/, plus a project-specific firewall allowlist and an explicit --dangerously-skip-permissions flag.
quote: network isolation with project-specific firewall allowlists ... claudebox --dangerously-skip-permissions ... Authentication state persists per-project in ~/.claudebox/<project-name>/.claude/

claim: Among cloud alternatives to local Docker for Claude Code, E2B uses Firecracker microVMs with ~150ms cold starts, Daytona uses Docker containers with industry-leading 27-90ms provisioning (raised $24M Series A Feb 2026), and Modal runs sandboxes on gVisor defined dynamically at runtime.
quote: Daytona achieves fastest provisioning—27-90ms from request to ready ... E2B has 150ms Firecracker cold starts ... Modal ... sandboxes running on gVisor

claim: Anthropic's official Agent SDK hosting guide names Modal Sandbox, Cloudflare Sandboxes, Daytona, E2B, Fly Machines, and Vercel Sandbox as supported cloud runtimes for hosting Claude Code agents.
quote: Claude Code agents can be hosted on multiple sandbox platforms including Modal Sandbox, Cloudflare Sandboxes, Daytona, E2B, Fly Machines, and Vercel Sandbox.

claim: The ghcr.io/anthropics/devcontainer-features/claude-code devcontainer feature silently overwrites any user-customized /usr/local/bin/init-firewall.sh because features run after the Dockerfile build, a known documented footgun.
quote: the devcontainer feature ... installs its own init-firewall.sh to /usr/local/bin/init-firewall.sh, which silently overwrites any user-customized version
